actions.name: Create Jira ticket
actions.description: Creates a Jira ticket for newly opened GitHub issues, syncing relevant details such as the issue title and number. It utilizes a GitHub Action to transfer information from GitHub to Jira, ensuring that issues are tracked in the project management system.

actions.name: Sync board list
actions.description: Updates the board JSON in the armbian/armbian.github.io repository based on changes to board configuration files. Sends a repository dispatch event to trigger further processing related to generating a build engine inventory.
actions.execution_method: push to main branch with changes in config/boards/*.*

actions.name: Sync labels
actions.description: Syncs labels from a YAML file to issues and pull requests based on changes in the specified file. It utilizes a labeler action to apply labels according to the definitions in '.github/labels.yml'. The process is executed conditionally based on the repository owner and the event type.
actions.execution_method: workflow_dispatch, push to main branch on .github/labels.yml, pull_request on .github/labels.yml

actions.name: Sync maintainers
actions.description: Syncs maintainers' information from a contacts database to board configuration files. Updates the BOARD_MAINTAINER property and opens a Pull Request if changes are detected. Identifies inactive maintainers and regenerates the CODEOWNERS file accordingly.

actions.name: Sync tools
actions.description: Updates tool versions in scripts by fetching the latest releases from specified GitHub repositories. Creates a pull request only if the version is actually updated, allowing for easy maintenance of tools that cannot be managed by dependabot. New tools can be added to the update process by modifying the defined matrix.
actions.cron_schedule: at 42 minutes past the hour, at 3:00, on day 16

actions.name: Dependency Review
actions.description: Scans dependency manifest files in pull requests to identify known-vulnerable package versions. Blocks merging of pull requests that introduce these vulnerabilities if the workflow run is marked as required. Audits outbound calls to enhance security during the process.

actions.name: Dispatch to fork
actions.description: Dispatches events to a forked repository based on various GitHub events like push, release, deployment, and pull requests. Utilizes a secret token to trigger a repository dispatch with details about the event, including the reference and SHA. Executes only on forked repositories to facilitate communication with the original repository.

actions.name: Mirror to Codeberg
actions.description: Pushes the contents of the repository to the Codeberg mirror, ensuring that the latest changes are reflected in the mirrored repository. It checks out the full history of the repository and uses a dedicated action to perform the mirroring operation securely with SSH authentication.

actions.name: Announce merge
actions.description: Announces merges to the main branch by sending a message to a Discord channel. It retrieves the merged commit details and formats a notification including the repository and actor information. The message is sent via a webhook using a POST request.

actions.name: Announce PR
actions.description: Announces pull requests labeled for maintenance on Discord for review, providing a summary of the PR and a link to it. Utilizes a webhook to send a formatted message including the repository and author details.

actions.name: Auto-label PR
actions.description: Automatically labels pull requests based on size, file categories, and creation date, while also removing the 'Ready to merge' label when updates occur. It categorizes PRs using a configuration file, assigns size labels based on the number of changed lines, and tracks quarterly labels based on the month of creation.

actions.name: Build artifacts
actions.description: Generates artifacts for pull requests labeled with 'Build' by authorized members of the 'Release manager' team. It checks the user's team membership and then compiles the artifacts if the user is authorized. The process includes a concurrency control to manage builds for the same pull request.

actions.name: Check board assets
actions.description: Verifies assets for newly added boards by checking for the existence of required board images and vendor logos in a specified repository. It identifies new board configuration files in a pull request and validates that the corresponding assets are present, generating comments for any missing items. The process ensures that all necessary assets are available for proper display in the Armbian Imager.

actions.name: Analyze kernel security
actions.description: Analyzes Linux kernel security options against hardening standards. Checks kernel configuration files for security issues and summarizes findings. Audits outbound calls for security compliance during the process.

actions.name: Clean workflow logs
actions.description: Cleans up workflow logs by deleting runs older than a specified number of days, helping to manage storage and maintain a tidy repository. It can be executed on a schedule or manually with input parameters for customization.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 0:00, on Mon

actions.name: Label PR on approval
actions.description: Labels pull requests as 'Ready to merge' upon approval and removes review-related labels. It downloads the pull request number from a previous workflow run and uses it to manage labels based on approval status. Additionally, it provides feedback through comments on the pull request.

actions.name: Lint scripts (post)
actions.description: Downloads diagnostic artifacts from the first phase of the lint pipeline and posts the findings as inline review comments and suggestion blocks on pull requests using reviewdog. It ensures that diagnostics are surfaced even if the initial linting fails, by synthesizing the necessary pull request event data without checking out the code. The workflow runs in the context of the BASE repository to maintain security while providing feedback on contributions from forks.

actions.name: Lint scripts
actions.description: Runs ShellCheck on shell scripts in a pull request to identify issues and provide auto-fix suggestions. Produces a pass/fail check on the PR based on critical findings and generates artifacts for further processing by a companion workflow. Outputs include findings and diffs for suggested fixes, which are posted inline on the PR via reviewdog.

actions.name: Listen PR review
actions.description: Saves the pull request number when a review is approved. It uploads the PR number as an artifact for further use. This helps in tracking and managing pull request reviews effectively.

actions.name: Rewrite kernel configs
actions.description: Rewrites kernel configurations by generating a dynamic matrix of boards and branches, executing the rewrite process, and aggregating changes into a single pull request. Collects modified files and summarizes changes, including additions and deletions, before uploading artifacts and creating a PR for review. Ensures synchronization of Linux kernel config fragments across different boards and families based on an inventory.
actions.cron_schedule: at 0 minutes past the hour, at 0:00, on MON

actions.name: Rewrite patches
actions.description: Rewrites kernel or U-Boot patches for a specified board and branch, updating the repository and checking for changes. If changes are detected, it creates a pull request with a summary of the modifications. The process includes cloning the repository, running a compilation script, and generating a detailed report of file changes.

actions.name: Security scan
actions.description: Runs a security analysis using Scorecards to assess the codebase's security posture and uploads the results to GitHub's code scanning dashboard. It also generates a SARIF file for the analysis results and optionally uploads it as an artifact. The analysis is performed weekly and on pushes to the main branch.
actions.cron_schedule: at 30 minutes past the hour, at 1:00, on Sat

actions.name: Validate board configs
actions.description: Validates changed board configuration files in pull requests by running a Python script that checks for errors and emits annotations on the PR diff. The process blocks merging if any validation errors are found. Only modified files are validated to optimize CI resources.

actions.name: Watchdog
actions.description: Monitors and reruns failed jobs for specified workflows, ensuring stability in CI/CD processes. It checks the latest run status and triggers a rerun if the previous attempt failed and hasn't exceeded the maximum retry limit. This helps maintain the reliability of automated workflows.

actions.name: Welcome first-time issue contributor
actions.description: Welcomes first-time issue contributors by sending a personalized message and applying a 'Good first issue' label. It enhances community engagement by encouraging contributors to stay informed and support the project. This process helps create a positive experience for new contributors.

actions.name: Welcome first-time PR contributor
actions.description: Welcomes first-time pull request contributors by sending a personalized message. It acknowledges their contribution and encourages further engagement with the project community. The message includes a link for sharing contact preferences and a reminder to star the repository.
